sections.label: Lyrics
lines: In the days to come, the mountain will rise,
Above the fear, beyond the cries,
Nations will stream through valleys wide,
To seek Your truth, to stand beside.
Your voice will teach the hearts of men,
To turn from war and dream again.
From Zion’s breath, Your rivers flow,
The peace You plant will surely grow.
And the Kingdom will stand, unbroken, unburned,
All empires fade, but Your glory returns,
From dust to dawn, through every age,
Your light will rule, no power can cage.
Unending, unfading
The Kingdom remains.
The swords will rust, the spears grow still,
The earth will rest beneath Your will.
Each soul beneath their fig and vine,
No fear, no thirst, just peace divine.
The songs once lost will rise again,
From hearts once scarred, restored within.
The nations learn Your gentle ways,
And justice burns with holy blaze.
And the Kingdom will stand, unbroken, unburned,
All empires fade, but Your glory returns,
Through flame and flood, through joy and pain,
Your love endures, Your truth remains.
Unending, unfading
The Kingdom remains.
Through kingdoms lost, through fading thrones,
Through shattered walls and scattered bones,
The saints inherit what You’ve decreed,
A reign of light no dark can seed.
The power passes, crowns decay,
But grace and mercy light the way.
A flame eternal, never dimmed,
A song of hope the stars have hymned.
All dominions fade like smoke in the wind,
But Yours, O Lord, begins and begins.
From heart to heart, from shore to shore,
Your mercy reigns forevermore.
And the Kingdom will stand, unbroken, unburned,
All empires fade, but Your glory returns,
Through time and trial, still unafraid,
Your promise holds; we are remade.
Unending, unfading
The Kingdom remains.
The mountain stands, the light won’t fade,
Hope in the Kingdom our hearts have made.
Not by might, nor by the flame
By love eternal, we rise again.
